paragraphs.text: As a consumer of digital services and software, I have been noting that more and more digital products require external servers, even though they are not necessary to fulfil their tasks. This is the case for online video games that are consumed, in medical products that require my family members to consume, neveres or cars that my friends have to be connected to the internet in order to perform certain advertised functions, etc. This makes consumers buy and do not own but merely subscribe to the services, and when the service or company closes the servers the consumer can no longer access or use what I buy. I consider this type of behaviour to be anti-consumer and create a dangerous precedent in the long run. This rule could do something to stop this before it is too late.
